items.claim: Cavanaugh possesses technical striking abilities, including explosive distance closing and a diverse body attack.
items.quote: Cavanaugh's technical striking is going to show out here. He's explosive and fast at closing distance. He's got nasty body kicks. He's got a nasty body attack with his hands, too.

items.claim: Brandon Royval is a step behind Cavanaugh on the feet.
items.quote: I think we're going to see Brandon Royval just a step behind on the feet

items.claim: Brandon Royval's best path to victory is landing high-impact strikes like spinning elbows or knees while entering range.
items.quote: I think it's going to be getting crazy. A Brandon Royval spinning elbow. A Brandon Royval just nasty knee up the center.

items.claim: Brandon Royval has never shown chin problems in his career.
items.quote: Brandon Royval has never shown chin problems before.

items.claim: Cory Sandhagen has improved his wrestling ability.
items.quote: I think with the vastly improved wrestling

items.claim: Mario Bautista lacks the knockout power to hurt Cory Sandhagen on the feet.
items.quote: doesn't necessarily have the knockout power in the spot. I don't see him hurting Sandhagen on the feet.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis utilizes a high-volume, pressure-based blitzing style with heavy body kicks.
items.quote: He's a southpaw who's got a very pressure-based game. It's high volume, it's consistent aggressive onslaught. It's a blitzing style.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett has an iron chin and will not be knocked out by Benoit Saint Denis's hands.
items.quote: he's got the iron scouse chin. He's not getting knocked out by Benoît Saint Denis with hands.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett is a higher-caliber grappler than Dan Hooker.
items.quote: Dan Hooker's ground game has always been mid. Paddy Pimblett is a high-caliber grappler.
